title: Replace regex smart folders with LLM-based grouping
description: User feedback: regex-based smart folder UI is terrible UX and they want LLM grouping instead. Replace the current rules builder with either (a) a plain-English description the LLM matches feeds against, or (b) one-shot auto-organize that clusters all feeds into folders.
close_reason: Replaced regex smart folder UI with two AI flows: auto-organize (clusters all feeds) and description-based smart folder (LLM matches feeds to a topic). Committed 7420bcb.

title: Phase 2: flat feed folders
description: Manual folders (flat, no nesting) to group feeds in sidebar. New folders table (id, name, sort_order). feeds.folder_id column. CRUD commands. Drag-drop + context menu to assign feed to folder.
close_reason: Phases 2+3 shipped: manual folders + smart folders with regex/URL/OPML-category rules

title: Phase 1: rename + remove feed
description: Right-click context menu on sidebar feed with Rename and Remove. Rename updates feeds.title. Remove deletes feed cascade; if any articles are starred, prompt user with count before deleting.
close_reason: Phase 1 shipped: rename inline + remove with starred-count confirm

title: Feedly OAuth login flow
description: Replace paste-token UX with OAuth login: localhost redirect listener, browser-launched authorization, token exchange, refresh-token handling. Settings stores client_id/secret.
close_reason: OAuth flow implemented; awaiting Feedly cred approval to bake in releases

title: Feedly sync: connect/disconnect commands + settings UI
description: Add connect_feedly, disconnect_feedly, get_feedly_status commands. Add Feedly connection section to Sync tab in SettingsDialog.

title: Feedly sync: bidirectional read/star state sync
description: Push read/star state changes to Feedly via tokio::spawn fire-and-forget.

title: Feedly sync: Feedly-aware feed refresh
description: Modify refresh_feed and refresh_all_feeds to use Feedly streams API for feeds with feedly_id. Update import_feedly to use stream fetcher.

title: Feedly sync: expand API client with streams, markers, tags
description: Add verify_token, fetch_stream_contents, feedly_entries_to_articles, mark_entries_read/unread, save/unsave_entry to feedly.rs

title: Feedly sync: add feedly_entry_id to articles schema
description: Add feedly_entry_id column to articles table, update Article model and all queries. Foundation for Feedly bidirectional sync.

title: Phase 3: smart folders
description: Rule-based auto-populated folders. folders.is_smart + rules_json. v1 rules: regex on feed title/url, OPML category match. AI classification in v2.
close_reason: Phases 2+3 shipped: manual folders + smart folders with regex/URL/OPML-category rules

value: Feed folders: flat structure, folders table (id, name, sort_order, is_smart, rules_json). feeds has folder_id + opml_category. Smart folder rules are JSON {mode: any|all, rules: [{type: regex_title|regex_url|opml_category, pattern|value}]}. Evaluated both backend (list_folders returns feed_count) and frontend (src/lib/smartFolder.ts).

Feedly OAuth: redirect URI is http://127.0.0.1:54321/feedly/callback (hardcoded port). Client_id/secret baked at build via FEEDLY_CLIENT_ID/FEEDLY_CLIENT_SECRET env vars using option_env!; falls back to user-entered creds in Settings. Feedly requires emailing partners@feedly.com to get creds, must obfuscate in binaries per their policy.
